snippets.title: Bayes' theorem from scratch in Python
snippets.code:
def bayes(p_b_given_a: float, p_a: float, p_b: float) -> float:
    """P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)"""
    if p_b <= 0:
        raise ValueError("P(B) must be > 0 to condition on B")
    return p_b_given_a * p_a / p_b

# Medical test: rare disease, positive result
p_disease = 0.001
p_pos_given_disease = 0.99
p_pos_given_healthy = 0.05

p_pos = p_pos_given_disease * p_disease + p_pos_given_healthy * (1 - p_disease)
p_disease_given_pos = bayes(p_pos_given_disease, p_disease, p_pos)

print(f"P(disease | positive) = {p_disease_given_pos:.4f}")  # ~0.019
snippets.explanation: Compute the denominator P(B) with the law of total probability, then apply the Bayes formula. Always guard against P(B) = 0.

snippets.title: Two-hypothesis partition with NumPy
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

# Hypotheses: H0 (fair coin), H1 (biased p=0.8)
priors = np.array([0.5, 0.5])
# Likelihoods P(7 heads in 10 flips | hypothesis)
likelihoods = np.array([0.117, 0.201])  # Binomial(10, p) for p=0.5 and p=0.8

evidence = likelihoods @ priors  # P(data) = sum_i P(data|Hi) P(Hi)
posteriors = likelihoods * priors / evidence

print("P(H0|data):", round(posteriors[0], 4))
print("P(H1|data):", round(posteriors[1], 4))
snippets.explanation: For multiple hypotheses, Bayes' theorem applies to each: P(Hᵢ|data) = P(data|Hᵢ)P(Hᵢ) / P(data). The evidence P(data) normalizes posteriors to sum to 1.

snippets.title: Spam/ham posterior with scikit-learn-style inputs
snippets.code:
def posterior_spam(p_spam: float, p_flag_given_spam: float, p_flag_given_ham: float) -> float:
    p_flag = p_flag_given_spam * p_spam + p_flag_given_ham * (1 - p_spam)
    return p_flag_given_spam * p_spam / p_flag

p_s = 0.01   # prior P(spam)
print(posterior_spam(p_s, 0.90, 0.05))  # P(spam | flagged) ~ 0.154
snippets.explanation: Encapsulate the total-probability denominator and Bayes update in one function—this is the core computation behind Naive Bayes classification.
